days.note: Afternoon stop — arriving from Nara, back to Kyoto

activities.name: Train: Nara → Uji
activities.notes: JR Nara Line from Nara Station to Uji Station — ~28 min, ¥420. Direct, no transfer. Uji sits between Nara and Kyoto on the same line — no backtracking.

activities.name: Byodoin Temple — Phoenix Hall
activities.notes: One of Japan's most iconic buildings — the Phoenix Hall sits on a small island reflected in the pond, exactly as depicted on the 10-yen coin. UNESCO World Heritage. The interior holds a large gilded Amitabha Buddha flanked by 52 flying bodhisattvas. ¥700 entry (grounds) + ¥300 for the hall interior. Afternoon is quieter than morning.

activities.name: Ujigami Shrine
activities.notes: The oldest surviving Shinto shrine in Japan (late Heian period, around 1060 AD) — a 10-min walk across the river from Byodoin. Modest and quiet, a stark contrast to Byodoin's grandeur. UNESCO World Heritage. Free entry.

activities.name: 🍵 Matcha at Tsuen Tea & Uji riverside
activities.notes: Uji is the matcha capital of Japan — the finest ceremonial-grade tea in the country comes from here. Tsuen Tea (通圓) has been operating since 1160 AD, making it one of the oldest tea houses in the world. Try matcha soba, matcha ice cream, and a proper bowl of whisked ceremonial matcha. Walk the riverside Tachibana-dori for street food and matcha-everything stalls.

activities.name: Uji River Walk
activities.notes: Stroll along the Uji River between the two bridges — one of the most scenic river walks in the Kyoto region. In May, fresh green (新緑) covers the hillsides. The river is famous for ancient cormorant fishing (Ukai) which runs June–September.

activities.name: Train: Uji → Kyoto
activities.notes: JR Nara Line from Uji Station back to Kyoto Station — ~17 min, ¥240.
